AT82.05 Artificial Intelligence:

Natural Language Understanding (NLU)

**Assignment 7: MCP-Server, AI Agent,** **and External Tool Integration**

**Student Name:** Saugat Shakya (st125986)

**Course:** AT82.05 Natural Language Understanding

**Date:** March 29, 2026

# **Overview**

This report documents the development of an integrated AI Agent ecosystem using the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The objective of Assignment 7 is to move beyond simple conversational AI and construct an agent capable of managing real-world tasks, specifically scheduling and calendar management through natural language understanding (NLU) and external tool integration.

The system was deployed locally using Docker and exposed to the internet via ngrok. The integrated ecosystem comprises the following components:

- MCP (Model Context Protocol) Server with multiple callable tools
- AI Agent capable of tool-based reasoning and contextual memory
- Telegram Bot interface for natural language user interaction
- Google Calendar integration for real-world event automation

Together, these components allow users to interact via natural language to create, query, and verify calendar events, demonstrating practical NLU applied to agentic AI workflows.

# **Task 1: MCP Infrastructure & AI Agent Setup**

## **1.1 Objective**

The objective of Task 1 is to establish the core MCP infrastructure: deploy an MCP Server exposing multiple internal tools, and connect a separate AI Agent workflow to consume those tools via the MCP Client interface. The agent must demonstrate the ability to reason over and invoke tools dynamically in response to natural language input.

## **1.2 Implementation Details**

The following steps were taken to implement the MCP infrastructure:

### **1.2.1 Server Deployment**

- n8n was deployed locally using Docker
- ngrok was used to expose the local n8n instance to a publicly accessible Production URL, enabling external API communications and webhook handling

### **1.2.2 MCP Server Workflow**

An n8n workflow was created to act as the MCP Server. The workflow is triggered by an MCP Server Trigger node and exposes three internal tools:

- Calculator - Performs arithmetic operations
- Date & Time - Retrieves the current date and time
- Code Tool - Executes text manipulation tasks (e.g., uppercase, reverse)

### **1.2.3 AI Agent Client Workflow**

A separate AI Agent workflow (Simple Client) was created with the following configuration:

- Model: Groq Chat Model (free API)
- Memory: Simple Memory for maintaining conversational context across turns
- Tool: MCP Client connected to the MCP Server's Production URL, allowing the agent to discover and invoke the server's tools

## **1.3 Workflow Diagrams**

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 1: MCP Server Workflow : MCP Server Trigger with three connected tools: Calculator, Date & Time, and Code Tool_

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 2: AI Agent (Simple Client) Workflow : Chat trigger connected to AI Agent with Groq Chat Model, Simple Memory, and MCP Client as tool_

## **1.4 Verification: AI Agent Using MCP Tools**

The AI Agent was tested using the n8n chat interface. The following interactions confirm that the agent successfully discovered and invoked the MCP Server's tools:

- Current time query : Agent used the Date & Time tool to return the current local time (14:43:39 UTC+7, March 29, 2026)
- Arithmetic calculation : Agent used the Calculator to compute 45 × 12 = 540
- Text transformation : Agent used the Code Tool to convert "hello" to "HELLO"

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 3: AI Agent chat demonstrating successful MCP tool usage: text reversal, time query, arithmetic calculation, and uppercase transformation_

## **1.5 Results**

The AI Agent successfully connected to the MCP Server, dynamically discovered the available tools, and invoked them appropriately in response to natural language queries. The agent maintained conversational context across multiple interactions using Simple Memory, demonstrating robust tool-based NLU capabilities.

# **Task 2: Telegram & Google Calendar Integration**

## **2.1 Objective**

Task 2 extends the AI Agent with a Telegram Bot interface and Google Calendar integration. The goal is to enable users to issue natural language commands via Telegram, which the agent interprets and uses to create and query Google Calendar events autonomously.

## **2.2 Implementation Details**

### **2.2.1 Telegram Bot Setup**

- A Telegram Bot was created using BotFather and connected to the n8n workflow via the Telegram Trigger node
- The Telegram Trigger listens for incoming messages and passes them to the AI Agent for processing
- The agent's responses are returned to the user via the "Send a text message" Telegram node

### **2.2.2 Google Calendar Integration**

Two Google Calendar tool nodes were integrated into the AI Agent:

- Create an event in Google Calendar : Enables the agent to create new calendar events with title, description, start time, and end time
- Get many events in Google Calendar : Enables the agent to query and verify existing events

### **2.2.3 Agent Configuration & Special Handling**

The AI Agent was configured with the following additional logic to ensure reliable calendar management:

- Future date enforcement: When a year is not specified by the user, the agent defaults to the nearest future occurrence (2026)
- Event field completeness: The agent ensures all created events include a title, description, and explicit start and end times
- Groq Chat Model was retained as the language model for low-latency responses
- Simple Memory provides conversational context so the agent can answer follow-up queries about previously created events

## **2.3 Workflow Diagram**

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 4: Telegram Client Workflow : Telegram Trigger connected to AI Agent (with Groq Chat Model and Simple Memory), two Google Calendar tool nodes, and Telegram 'Send a text message' output node_

## **2.4 Verification: Automated Project Scheduling**

The agent was tested via Telegram by issuing a single natural language command to create four project phase events in Google Calendar. The following events were requested and successfully created:

| **Event**                     | **Date**       | **Time**     | **Description**       |
| ----------------------------- | -------------- | ------------ | --------------------- |
| 1st Phase: Literature Review  | April 1, 2026  | 10:00 -11:00 | Review related papers |
| 2nd Phase: Project Proposal   | April 8, 2026  | 10:00 -11:00 | Prepare proposal      |
| 3rd Phase: Update Progress    | April 15, 2026 | 10:00 -11:00 | Present progress      |
| 4th Phase: Final Presentation | April 22, 2026 | 10:00 -11:00 | Final demo            |

_Table 1: Project phase events created via Telegram in Google Calendar_

## **2.5 Screenshots**

### **Telegram Interaction**

The following screenshot shows the complete Telegram conversation, including the command to create all four events and subsequent verification queries:

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 5: Telegram conversation: user commands event creation and verifies individual phase dates via natural language queries; agent confirms each accurately_

### **Google Calendar Confirmation**

The following screenshot confirms that all four project phase events were successfully created in Google Calendar with the correct titles, dates, and times:

![](data:image/jpeg;base64,)

_Figure 6: Google Calendar (April 2026) displaying all four project phases: Literature Review (Apr 1), Project Proposal (Apr 8), Update Progress (Apr 15), and Final Presentation (Apr 22), each at 10:00-11:00_

## **2.6 Results**

The Telegram-integrated AI Agent successfully:

- Received and interpreted natural language scheduling commands via Telegram
- Created all four project phase events in Google Calendar with correct titles, descriptions, dates, and times
- Responded to follow-up natural language queries, accurately confirming event details (e.g., "what do I have on 22nd April?", "when is the 2nd phase?")
- Maintained conversational context across multiple verification queries within the same session

# **Conclusion**

This assignment demonstrates a fully functional, end-to-end AI Agent ecosystem built on n8n, integrating the Model Context Protocol (MCP), Groq LLM, Telegram, and Google Calendar. The system successfully exhibits:

- Tool-based agentic reasoning : The AI Agent dynamically selects and invokes the appropriate MCP tool based on user intent
- Natural language understanding : Free-form user inputs are correctly parsed and mapped to structured API calls (calendar event creation, retrieval)
- Multi-channel integration : The system bridges Telegram (user interface), n8n (orchestration), and Google Calendar (external service)
- Memory and context retention : Simple Memory enables coherent multi-turn conversations across tool invocations

The implementation validates that MCP-based architectures enable modular, extensible AI agent systems capable of real-world task automation through natural language interfaces.
